input.prompt:
Locked camera, full-frame view of a hand-drawn pencil and watercolor portrait on cream sketchbook paper. The portrait is of a young boy.

PAGE-TURN TRANSITION:

[0.0 – 0.8s]: Hold on the child portrait. Subtle stillness.

[0.8 – 1.5s]: The right edge of the page begins to LIFT and curl gently upward, as if a hand or breeze is turning the page. The child portrait starts to peel away from the right side.

[1.5 – 3.0s]: The page continues to TURN — the child portrait curls up and over toward the left, sliding out of the frame. Underneath, a new page is revealed: the next portrait emerges into view as the previous page lifts away. The new portrait is the same person but older — a young man in his late teens with a wide genuine smile, in pencil and watercolor on cream paper.

[3.0 – 4.5s]: The previous page finishes turning out of frame to the left. The new portrait (late-teen) settles flat into view, filling the frame.

[4.5 – 5.0s]: Hold on the late-teen portrait, matching the end frame exactly.

Critical: this is a PAGE-TURN motion — like turning the next page in a sketchbook. The previous portrait curls away physically; the new one is revealed underneath. NOT a morph, NOT a fade, NOT a dissolve. Real paper-page-turning physics.

Constraints: locked camera, no zoom, no pan. NO visible hand or fingers — the page turns on its own. Single continuous take. The clip MUST end exactly at the provided end frame for seamless stitching.
